title: Energy module
abstract: The present invention relates to an energy module (100), where said Energy Module (100) comprises of cell holder casing (101), external cover (106), plurality of Li ion cylindrical cell (200), current collector strip (108) which is attached to each other with the connecting strip (104). Further, the cylindrical cell has two portion negative (200a) and positive terminal (200b) where the elastic member based electrical connector system (105) is connected to at least one terminal of the plurality of Li ion cylindrical cells (200) with the rivet. The current collector strip (108) is connected to the positive terminal (200a) of the cylindrical cell (200) with the rivet (103) thereby, ensures the ease of accessibility and releasing of the gases from the cylindrical cell (200) in the thermal runaway condition.
claims:
Claims We Claim; 1. An Energy Module (100) for a powered unit comprising of: A plurality of cells (200); A current collector strips (108); A plurality of rivet (102, 103); Said pair of current collector strip (108) is mounted on two terminals (200a, 200b) of said plurality of cells (200); and Said plurality of cells (200) consists of said two terminals (200a, 200b), where plurality of elastic member based electrical connector system (105) is connected to said at least one terminal of said plurality cells (200) with rivet (102). 2. A elastic member based electrical connector system (105) for an energy module (100), said elastic member based electrical connector comprising; an upper ring (105a), a middle coil (105b), and a lower disc (105c); said lower disc (105c) connected to said current collector strips (108) with a rivet (102) and made of positive temperature coefficient material. 3. The Energy Module (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein said at least one terminal (200a) of said plurality of cells (200) is connected to said current collector strips (108) with a rivet (103). 4. The energy module (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein said plurality of cells (200) are covered with a protective shield (107), where said shield (107) is mounted between a cell holder (101) and an external cover (106). 5. The energy module (100) as claimed in claim 3, wherein said protective shield (107) is slidable from at least one side of said plurality of cell (200) 6. The energy module (100) as claimed in claim 3, wherein said plurality of cell holder casing (101) is configured with slots (110) to accommodate separate said plurality of n cells (200). 7. The energy module (100) as claimed in claim 3, wherein said cell holder casing (101) has 

top portion (101b) and bottom portion (101a), where said top portion (101b) and said bottom portion (101a) is configured with holes (112) to attached said current collector strip (108) with said rivet (102, 103). 8. The energy module (100) as claimed in claim 1 or claim 2, wherein said upper ring (105a) is connected to said another terminal (200b) of said plurality of cell (200) said middle coil (105b) acts as a shock absorber. 9. An energy module (100) for a powered unit, comprising of; a cell holder casing unit (101); said cell holder casing (101) includes a pair of separate compartments (101a% 101bâ) to accommodate at least a stack of cell (200); 10. The energy module (100) as claimed in claim 9, wherein said cell holder casing (101) is covered by a pair of end cover (401a, 401b) with various attachment means. 11. A method for venting out the gases from energy module comprising: Stacking of cells in the cell holder casing covered with the external cover having venting holes; connecting an electrical connector having elastic member with rivet to at least one terminal of the plurality of cells; compressing the elastic member based electrical connector system connected to at least one terminal of the plurality of cells to create a venting path for each cell; releasing of gases through the holes present on the external cover of energy module;
